observations.managementAction: The LGU is desirous to comply with the recommendations of the Audit Team, however, due to the pandemic they are having delays in complying with the requirements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive require the Acting Municipal Accountant to track the cause of the discrepancy between the SL vis-a-vis the GL of the “Due to NGA” account and prepare necessary subsidiary ledgers, if warranted.

observations.managementAction:
The LGU officials informed the Audit Team of the following actions that they will be taking to address the recommendations:

The required CBMS and training on its usage was one of the PPAs which was realigned and replaced in CY 2020 to give way to PPAs that will address COVID-19, on this juncture, we will make sure that on the following year it will still be included in the fund’s plan and budget; and

The revised 2021 GAD Plan will include training on gender analysis using the HGDG Tool for the correct attribution of the GAD Budget and Accomplishment.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated and Management agreed with our recommendations that the Local Chief Executive direct the General Services Officer (GSO) to comply with the requirements of COA Circular 2018-002 on the appraisal and preparation of Property Inventory Form (PIF), among others, to be used in the determination of premium to be paid.

In addition, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive 

(a) given the limited resources, provide a reasonable budget for the payment of premiums, giving priority to those exposed to highest risk of loss/damage thru fortuitous events properties; and 

(b) instruct the Municipal Assessor to fast track the assessment of the above-defined properties in aid of the requirements for the calculation of premiums.

observations.managementAction: The HRMO, has apprised the Audit Team that there is an existing request from the Assessor’s Office, and recently, one job order (January 25, 2021) was transferred and one permanent item was issued last February 16, 2021 to their office to address their request.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our previous year’s recommendations that the Local Chief Executive:

consider, if budget permits, the provision for an increase in pay of the existing JOs earning below the minimum wage; and 

instruct the HRMO to (i) continue looking for possible candidates from the array of job order employees for permanency based on available vacant positions for recommendation to the Personnel Selection Board (PSB) of the Municipality to reduce the number of job orders, taking into consideration the mandated PS limitation under the Local Government Code; and

(ii) evaluate the necessity of the hired job orders determined doing redundant works and conduct spot checking if needed, to verify actual services performed.
observations.managementAction:
The HRMO has updated the Audit Team that continuous coordination with the Sangguniang Bayan is being made for the abolishment and creation of permanent positions in order to promote deserving employees, taking consideration the PS limitation. For CY 2020, 10, 24, 25 positions were abolished, created and filled up, respectively. 

In addition, the HRMO has coordinated with Department Heads concerned on overlapping of duties/work assignments and recommended that they provide them with other activities that the public would benefit more from their services. Further, the supposed decrease in Job order was not realized despite the permanency of previous JO owing to the exigency of service, request for manpower to be assigned in the isolation facility/temporary monitoring facility for suspected COVID-19 individuals and to the environment office. 

The hiring was also due to the retirement of personnel, the needed preparation to assume the responsibilities of the retiree/the promotion of the personnel to the retirees position was needed to ensure the continuity of public service in the LGU.

Relatedly, the Budget Officer expressed his intention that if budget permits, provide for the increase in the compensation of the job orders in order to match the mandated minimum wage.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our prior years’ recommendations and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:
instruct the GSO to (i) conduct periodic inventory of supplies as prescribed by the above regulation; and (ii) make an effort to procure directly from the DBM-PS all anticipated common-use supplies and resort to local suppliers only upon unavailability of supplies needed; and
require the Municipal Accountant to (i) ensure that all purchases of supplies and materials are recorded under the proper inventory account; (ii) prepare supplies ledger cards for delivered items as called for in the above-quoted regulation; and (iii) examine noted deviations in item 1.23. of this memorandum, for future similar transactions, adhere closely with the requirements of the law, preceding to payment thereof.

On recorded representation expenses related to COVID-19 - ₱927,549.74
Recorded representation expenses with an aggregate value of ₱927,549.74 were observed to have dispensed with the meals served to COVID-19 patients under quarantine in the isolation facility and personnel assigned in the vaccination facility, food assistance to affected families in the LGU.
Of the total disbursements as mentioned above, it showed insufficient supporting documents contrary to Section 4 of PD 1445 which stipulates that, claims against government funds shall be supported with complete documentation. Common deficiencies noted on these disbursements were the same as those mentioned.
We reiterated our previous year’s recommendations and made additions  and the Local Chief Executive agreed to consider creating an internal audit unit/services which will oversee the completeness of the documentation of the transactions prior to payment in conjunction with the rules under Local Budget Circular 110 which require the Municipal Accountant to strictly enforce “no processing of disbursements” if with incomplete documentations and approval of required signatories.

We reiterated our recommendation and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Acting-Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRS) of all bank accounts presenting complete reconciling items such as outstanding checks, unrecorded deposits, cancelled checks, bank charges and error/s, if any.
We also recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to follow-up the request for bank statement to the concerned branch to aid in the preparation of the BRS.

We reiterated our prior years recommendation that the Local Chief Executive require the Acting Municipal Accountant to track the cause of the discrepancy between the SL vis-a-vis the GL of the “Due to NGAs” account and prepare the necessary subsidiary ledgers.
We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the:
implementing departments and the Bids and Awards Committee (i) to strictly adhere to the agreement or guidelines relative to the specific fund held in trust; and (ii) strictly adhere to the provision of R.A. No. 9184 in the procurement of goods and services; and
instruct all departments concerned to submit noted deficient documents as mentioned in item 3.9. of this Memorandum.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendations and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to:
request the Sangguniang Bayan to prepare a clear-cut guideline or adopt the policies and procedures aligned with the Department of Social Welfare and Development (DSWD) Memorandum Circulars;
require the Municipal Budget Officer (MBO) to provide adequate funding to support their operations, for “Aid to Barangays” so as to facilitate fund transfers instead of a dole-out donation.
We also recommended that the LCE instruct the:
MBO to refrain from charging financial assistance, donations and subsidies to appropriations not allotted for such; and
Acting Municipal Accountant to (i) record assistance, donations, and subsidies on the appropriate account based on COA Circular 2015-009; and (ii) check the completeness of the required supporting documents for every transaction before any payment is made.

We reiterated our recommendations that the Local Chief Executive:
if budget permits, provide a service vehicle intended only for the Assessor’s Office to facilitate the immediate dissemination/distribution of Notices of Assessment (NoA) to households; and
require the Municipal Assessor to set attainable yearly targets in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer and implement the proposed strategies to increase and improve collection and to minimize the deficiency in Basic/SEF collection as they have mentioned above.
Management comment: The Audit Team was apprised that the strategies formulated during the Executive Legislative Agenda for the year 2022, involve the Barangay Officials in collecting RPT by granting other incentives to Barangay who reached their total quota collections for the year.
Likewise, the recommendation of COA to set attainable yearly targets will be set by the Municipal Assessor in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer, based on tax potentials of tax delinquencies in their respective barangays.
Intensify the Task Force to Conduct Tax Information dissemination in 33 Barangays and put up an extension office in Barangay Bahay Pare and Barangay Salapungan Hall on scheduled date.
Finally, during the exit conference, the LCE has instructed the MBO to allot funds for the purchase of the service vehicle.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendations and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:
direct the General Services Officer (GSO) to continue with the compliance made with COA Circular 2018-002 on the appraisal and preparation of Property Inventory Form (PIF), among others, to be used in the determination of premium to be paid;
include in the budget the provision for insurance premiums, giving priority to those exposed to highest risk of loss/damage thru fortuitous events; and
instruct the Municipal Assessor to give precedence to the assessment of the above-defined properties in aid of the requirements for the calculation of premiums.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendations and made additions thereto and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:
ensure that all disbursing/special disbursing officers are promptly applied for renewal of bonds upon expiration;
remind all concerned personnel to submit approved payroll/budget estimates prior to the request for cash advances to substantiate the transaction; and
instruct the Acting-Municipal Accountant to check the completeness of the supporting documents prior to payment particularly on the submission of payroll or budget estimates for every request of cash advance.
Management comment: Delayed renewal of bonding of disbursing officers was due to the transition from manual to online Fidelity Bonding System of the Bureau of Treasury.
During the exit conference, the Municipal Treasurer apprised the Audit Team that focal point person assigned in the renewal of bonds has her own monitoring of lapsing bonds. However, as cited above, the automated system of renewal caused the delay.

We reiterated and made supplemental recommendations and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:
direct the MPDO to (i) henceforth, check/monitor if actual projects undertaken from the fund are in harmony with the approved AIP; and (ii) coordinate efforts with the BAC members and Municipal Engineer to properly plan and schedule all procurement activities and conduct regular monitoring of the status of project implementation so that necessary interventions may be introduced at the earliest time to give way for their timely completion; and (iii) present the PPAs in the Annual Investment Program (AIP) charged under the 20% DF in detail;
instruct the BAC to consider early procurement activities prior to the approval of the appropriation short of award, in order to facilitate the required bidding and other procurement activities on time; and
advise the Municipal Budget Officer (MBO) (i) look into the noted erroneous charging made, to avert similar lapses that may have legal implications in the future; and (ii) for the inclusion of an allotment for those ineligible charges made under appropriate budget accounts, to maximize the Fund for its intended purpose.

We reiterated previous years audit recommendations and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the LDRRMO, (i) together with the LDRRMC, to align LDRRM Plan for CY 2021 and thereafter with the LDRRMFIP prescribed by the above-quoted regulations; (ii) submit supplemental/amended AIP showing PPAs that were reprogrammed; and (iii) regularly monitor the implementation of projects out of the fund so as to avoid implementation of PPAs which are more appropriately chargeable against the General Fund.
We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to (i) strictly observe the correct usage of funds particularly the allotments in the approved LDRRMFIP, AIP and the Appropriation Ordinance; and (ii) henceforth, refrain from charging expenditures which were inconsistent with allotment and/or do not have direct impact on disaster risk reduction and management.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendations that the Local Chief Executive:
if budget permits, purchase the required CBMS and the training on the usage thereof;
require the Municipal Planning and Development Officer (MPDO), in coordination with the GAD Focal Point Person (GFPP), to work on the establishment of a complete GAD database and the maintenance thereof for a more gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation; and
require the GFPP to (i) use the HGDG tool in the attribution of the GPB or allot a separate fund for GAD; (ii) closely monitor the implementation of the programs and projects embodied in the GPB and if needed, intervene immediately, if delays in the implementation were observed, amend the GPB, if warranted.
Management comment: As part of the future action of the LGU to comply with the Audit Team’s recommendations a CBMS training required will be included among the listed PPAs to be proposed in the Comprehensive Development Plan for 2023-2028 and other related documents to ensure its funding and implementation.
Efforts to improve the GAD database will also be taken into consideration to ensure a more responsive planning and programming.
The LGU has allotted 5% separate GAD fund for 2022 as mandated in the Municipal GAD Code.
Finally, close monitoring of PPAs in the GPB will be observed thru the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) quarterly meetings.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendations that the Local Chief Executive:
direct the Municipal Budget Officer, to consider, if budget permits, the provision for an increase in pay of the existing JOs earning below the minimum wage; and
instruct the HRMO to (i) continue looking for possible candidates from the array of job order employees for permanency based on available vacant positions for recommendation to the Personnel Selection Board (PSB) of the Municipality to reduce the number of job orders, taking into consideration the mandated PS limitation under the Local Government Code; and (ii) evaluate the necessity of the hired job orders in order to reduce the amount of expenses coupled therein.
Management comment: The Management agreed on the recommendation to continue looking for possible candidates from the array of job order employees and have apprised the Audit Team, that the human resource department continuously coordinates with the Local Chief Executive, Office of the Sangguniang Bayan and Municipal Budget Officer regarding ordinances for the creation of positions that would allow existing Job orders to qualify for permanency, taking consideration the mandated PS limitation.
In connection, the human resource department has evaluated job orders to reduce the amount of expenses and coordinated with departments concerned on overlapping of work assignments and advised transfer to other offices and likewise the recommendation to the LCE for the termination of contracts of those JOs found to have not performed their duties.
Based on records, there was an increase on the number of JOs due to the LGUs COVID-19 response where they were assigned in COVID Ward, vaccination teams as encoders and consultant doctors with expertise in COVID-19.
Lastly, during the exit conference, the LCE had emphasized that due to the pandemic a lot of individuals were displaced and as a help, he employed their services for a short period and an update was given to the Audit Team that a reduction of the number is set in July 2022.

We reiterated and supplemented our previous year’s recommendations and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed to, in coordination with the Designated-MENRO:
continue to intensify and sustain the current information drive and awareness campaign among the residents, in coordination with the Barangay, following the strategies identified under the approved 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan of the LGU;
request the Barangays (i) to enhance/adapt the Municipal ordinances pertaining to the solid waste through the creation of a schedule on the frequency of waste collection and installation of garbage bins on pick-up points for each barangay to encourage proper segregation of waste; and (ii) without Materials Recovery Facility (MRF) to exert effort in locating appropriate MRF or, if possible, request other Barangays with MRF for the clustering scheme as allowed under the law;
allot a budget or request from the National Government Agencies the required funding for the concreting of the road, rehabilitation of the MRF, and purchase of transformer to support the operation of the idle equipment of the LGU, in aid of the volume reduction of waste; and
exercise strong political will to implement the applicable ordinances pertaining to Solid Waste Management.
We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to properly monitor charges to the allotted fund and refrain from charging ineligible expenses thereto; and
consider creating a separate department for the MENRO in order to address the budgetary constraints in the implementation of programs addressing the waste management issues of the Municipality.
Management comment: the MENRO Team, in coordination with the GSO and Barangay Council, will continue to pursue the awareness campaign on proper segregation and disposal of wastes among the residents/constituents.
The issue was discussed during the joint meeting last June 21, 2022. Prior to the joint meeting, our team, MENRO coordinated with the chairman of each barangay, that our garbage collectors will only be collecting the trash on their specified pick up points, “per sitio” effective September 2022 to give them enough time to prepare. Our staff were assigned to daily monitor the cleanliness of every barangay. Proper schedule for the monitoring was made.
Finally, during the exit conference, the LCE has directed the Municipal Budget Officer and the Human Resource Officer to check on the budget limitation and requirements of the establishment of a Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Office with a department head and three admin staff as per CSC requirements.

We reiterated and supplemented our prior years’ audit recommendations that the Local Chief Executive:
in close coordination with the Treasurers’ Office and Accounting Department, (i) make sure that the same amount withheld is remitted the following month; (ii) look into the breakdown of the variances from the withheld amount and remittances made on previous years and make necessary adjustments, if warranted; (iii) and consistently observe the deadline for the remittance of the mandated deductions to avoid the incurrence of interest and penalties;
as part of the internal control system, designate/appoint a person who will check all deductions and remittances made to make sure all are accounted for; and
instruct the Acting Municipal Accountant to maintain a complete Index of Payments for proper monitoring of claims and for prompt and accurate recording and remittances to GSIS, PhilHealth and Pag-IBIG.
Management comment: The COA recommendation to comply with the required withholding and remittances of the mandated deductions such as Pag-IBIG, PhilHealth and GSIS contribution is well noted and taken. At this point in time, we look into the breakdown of the variances from the withheld amount and remittances made in this office and we found that the discrepancies between the amounts withheld and remitted were due to the deductions of newly appointed personnel who do not have identification numbers for GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth and they have increase in premiums but not reflected first on the GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth online system.  The discrepancies compiled and remitted in full at the time their identification numbers posted in the online system of the GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth.

We reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendation that the Local Chief Executive direct the Acting-Municipal Accountant to (a) look into the cause of the error in the remittance made and make the adjustments/amendments to BIR returns, if necessary, to reflect the actual deductions based on specific reference months; and (b) henceforth, ensure that taxes withheld are remitted intact to the BIR.

We restated our prior year’s recommendation and the Local Chief Executive agreed to continue efforts to collect receivables originating from the said disallowance, by seeking legal action against the rest of the accountable persons still alive, they being solidarily liable for the disallowance.

We reiterated and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive:

hire additional personnel in the Accounting Department, specifically the Accounting Department Head position who will address all issues pertaining to accounting deficiencies as mentioned above; and
instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Acting Municipal Accountant to look into the noted lapses as mentioned above such as the unrecorded checks, non-reversal of stale checks, among others, and prepare adjusting/correcting entries if necessary and reconcile at least quarterly the cashbook Cash in Bank and accounting records.

We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive advise the Municipal Treasurer to 

(i) make a follow-up to those barangays with long outstanding unremitted CTCs until the same are remitted; and 

(ii) enforce to BTs the required frequency of remittance to avert the existence of long outstanding unremitted CTCs issued to Barangays to make sure that they are properly recorded and remitted on the year when they are actually collected in compliance with the accrual method of accounting.

We reiterated our previous recommendation and made additional recommendations to the Local Chief Executive to:

request for the passing of the required resolution to reactivate the account with the SB; 

instruct the Municipal Treasurer to reactivate the account; and

direct the Municipal Treasurer and Acting-Municipal Accountant to determine appropriate disposition of the remaining balance of the bank account such as the return of the excess fund to the source agency, if warranted.
observations.managementAction: The LGU has passed the required resolution last February 10, 2021 thru SB Resolution No. 175, series 2021, for the reactivation of the account. As of this writing, the request for the reactivation was already submitted to the depository bank of the LGU, however, the bank temporarily ceased operation owing to the COVID-19 cases, hence the request was still unaddressed.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive constitute a task force/team that will determine circumstances behind the inexistent Time Deposit account; and work on finding/production of necessary documents in order to determine the persons accountable for the subject transaction.

We also recommended that if circumstances warrant, request for the derecognition of the asset as there are no future economic benefits that can be derived therefrom, to be able to cleanse the records.

We reiterated our last year’s recommendations and made complementary recommendations that the Local Chief Executive: 

ensure that all special disbursing officers are formally designated as required by the above-mentioned regulation; and accordingly, secure adequate fidelity bond; 

remind all concerned personnel to submit approved budget estimates prior to the request for cash advances to substantiate the transaction; and

instruct the Acting-Municipal Accountant to check the completeness of the supporting documents prior to payment.
observations.managementAction: Due to COVID-19 pandemic and unexpected circumstances, wanting to expedite the service to our constituents we overlooked the essential documents needed to support the cash advance. Moreover, the Acting-Municipal Accountant has argued that he is continuously checking the completeness of the supporting documents for every transaction.

observations.recommendation:
We commended the Management for its commitment to serve its constituents through this program offered by the National Government.

Likewise, we recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive direct the Local Finance Committee to strictly adhere to the guidelines affiliated with the grants given by the National Government.

We reiterated our prior years’ recommendation that the Local Chief Executive:

instruct the GSO to 

(i) prepare the stock cards for the purchased supplies; 

(ii) conduct periodic inventory of supplies as prescribed by the above regulation; and 

(iii) make an effort to consistently procure directly from the DBM-PS all anticipated common-use supplies and resort to local suppliers only upon unavailability of supplies needed,  as certified by a DBM official; and 

require the Acting Municipal Accountant to record purchases under the proper inventory account and prepare the supplies ledger cards and Procurements Received Journal for delivered items as called for in the above-quoted regulation.
observations.managementAction: The LGU was unable to procure with the PS-DBM due to the pandemic, likewise, some of the supplies needed were not available on their stock.

intensify and sustain the current information drive and awareness campaign among the residents, in coordination with the Barangay, following the strategies identified under the approved 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan of the LGU;

observations.managementAction:
The LGU has apprised the Audit Team that last March 1, 2021, the MENRO had a meeting with the barangays, regarding the strict enforcement of the “No collection, no segregation” policy of the LGU. Likewise, their personnel had distributed leaflets to their constituents for information dissemination and to educate them on the proper segregation of waste.

Similarly, the current statistics of the Barangay MRF disclosed that, there are 25 out of the 33 barangays that have Barangay MRF, and the remaining 8 are still looking for a lot fit for the establishment thereof.

Relatedly, as of March 1, 2021, the Environment Office are awaiting for the approval of request for the construction of the roadway and rehabilitation of the Central MRF of the Municipality.

(iii) as part of internal control system, designate a person who will check all deductions and remittances made to make sure all are accounted for.

We reiterated our recommendation and made additions and Management agreed, that the Local Chief Executive direct the Acting-Municipal Accountant to

(a)	instruct the Acting-Municipal Treasurer to reactivate the account; and

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

(b)	facilitate the required procedures pertaining to the balance of the bank account such as return of the excess fund to the source agency, if warranted. Additionally, prepare Bank Reconciliation Statements and submit the same to the Audit Team within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction:
The LCE had already requested the Sanggunian Bayan Members to amend the previous resolution and authorizing the newly designated Acting Municipal Treasurer to facilitate the activation, closing and transfer of the Special Trust Account No. 0580-036819-080 on the DBP San Fernando Branch to the regular Trust Fund Account No. 00-0-05284-580-9. 

Upon approval of the resolution, the Acting Municipal Treasurer will facilitate the activation, closing and transfer of the Special Trust Account No. 0580-036819-080 on the DBP San Fernando Branch to the regular Trust Fund Account No. 00-0-05284-580-9.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive initiate the conduct of a verification to determine the circumstances behind the inexistent Time Deposit account to reflect a reliable balance in the financial statements of the Municipality.

observations.managementAction:
The HRMO is aware about the presence of needed manpower in the office of the Municipal Engineer, GSO and Municipal Accountant’s Office but due to the fact that we have issues regarding over hiring of job orders prioritization has been made in the distribution of manpower.

The engineering office submitted the inventory of local road networks in this municipality based on all the records found in our office as of March 5, 2020.

Moreover, the accounting office will start this year the preparation of Property Ledger Cards, the maintenance of subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network as recommended by COA.
Likewise, the GSO agreed to the audit observation and will exert more effort to fulfill the recommendation.

observations.recommendation:

(a)	consider creating an internal audit unit/services which will oversee the completeness of the documentation of the transactions prior to payment in conjunction with the rules on its establishment under Local Budget Circular 110 with subject Internal Audit Manual 2016. That will intensify monitoring if (i) all claims for travel are within the allowed limit as prescribed by EO 77; (ii) certifications of unavailability of funds from the Barangay or Schools requesting for financial assistance is attached to the request; and (iii) common deficiencies on transactions as mentioned in item 1.13 are henceforth complied prior to payment;
(b)	ensure that all special disbursing officers has a formal designation as such as required by the above-mentioned regulation; and
(c)	require the officials concerned to submit the required documentation of the transaction cited under item 1.2., for further evaluation as basis for the Audit Team’s subsequent audit action.

instruct the Acting Municipal Accountant to (i) continue its compliance with the preparation and maintenance of Property Ledger Cards; (ii) keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components tor every road network; and (iii) prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation tor each component at the end of the year; and
advise the General Services Officer to (i) comply with the preparation and maintenance of Property Cards; (ii) prepare an Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property and Report of Waste Materials, where applicable, for properties and request the same for disposal; (iii) facilitate the conversion of vehicles with green plates to red plates and follow-up with concerned agencies the availability of government plates for newly acquired service vehicles.

We reiterated our previous years’ recommendations that the Local Chief Executive, in coordination with the Acting-MENRO:

Similarly, the current statistics of the Barangay MRF disclosed that, there are 25 out of the 33 barangays that have Barangay MRF, and the remaining 8 are still looking for a lot fit for the establishment thereof.
Relatedly, as of March 1, 2021, the Environment Office are awaiting for the approval of request for the construction of the roadway and rehabilitation of the Central MRF of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the:

(a)	Chief of Hospital, in coordination with the Administrative Officer, to (i) assign a point person who will handle and monitor the issuance of charge slips to various end-user units, he shall identify and record the missing number series prior to the issuance and receipt of the end user; (ii) set-up accountabilities of the issuer of charge slips to ensure that they shall be held accountable for any loss thereof; (iii) formulate measures (in writing) to deter the presence of absconding patients; (iv) follow procedures on supplies inventory management procedures such as preparation of Stock Cards, among others; (v) continue with the efforts on preparation/dissemination of demand letters to clients with outstanding balances, copy furnished the Accounting Department; and (vi) adhere closely on the requirements of DOH pertaining to segregation of waste and providing dietary services to the Infirmary; and
(b)	Sanggunian Bayan to pass an ordinance pertaining to grants of discounts and mark-up on medicines sold as mentioned above.
observations.managementAction:
The Officials of CMI reported the following planned course of actions pertaining to the audit observations:

a)To prevent any further missing number from the series of charge slips that is not accounted for, the following procedures will be done:

i.	The cashier and CSR will be assigned in the maintenance of charge slips. They will count blank charge slips before releasing to personnel in charge and take note of the series numbers of the charge slips that were released. They shall record any missing number series prior to release.
ii.	The issuer shall issue charge slips in triplicate using a carbon paper to ensure that data is replicated properly without anything added or removed. The three copies will go to the issuer, municipal office treasurer’s office and the cashier.
iii.	A monthly reconciliation of issued and used charge slips will be done and investigation of missing series numbers that was not recorded before will be done.
iv.	If there is no valid and relevant explanation as to why there is a missing charge slip from the charge slips that were issued (excluding the missing number series that was already recorded), the issuer of the charge slip shall be liable for the loss of that specific number.
v.	An incident report will be made for all the missing charge slips.

b)To deter the cases of absconding, the following procedures will be observed moving forward:

i.	For non-emergency cases, the patient is required to surrender his/her ID and log in before getting treated.
ii.	If in the event that he/she has no ID in his/her being, the ID of his/her representative shall be surrendered instead.
iii.	If in the event that he/she has no representative or representative has no ID, a log in will be done and patient information sheet will be signed.
iv.	After payment of all dues and charges, the patient or representative will present receipt signed by cashier to the person in charge of IDs and patient information sheet.
•	If ID was provided, this shall be returned 
•	If a log in and patient information sheet was signed, the person in charged countersigns the log book to indicate that the person has paid all dues and charges and that he/she is free from any liability on that date. 
v.	For emergency cases, the representative will surrender his/her ID and log in. If he/she has no ID, steps 3 and 4 will follow. 
vi.	The person in charge of the IDs and log book with be the guard on duty while the one in charge of the patient information sheet will be the nurse on duty.
vii.	In case that patient does abscond, the hospital will have his/her ID or his/her information. The liaison will notify the barangay captain of this case and this shall be forwarded to him/her.

c)An inventory management will be placed. Instructions will be given to the staffs that will be in charge of this. The following will be observed: 

i.	Stock codes will be assigned to inventory.
ii.	An inventory count will be done to establish a beginning inventory of all stock items. The beginning unit price will also be determined. A report on the beginning inventory will be forwarded to Accountant.
iii.	The requisition and issue slip will be filled out by department that received or issued stocks.
iv.	For every issuance and receipt of stock in inventory, this will be recorded using a stock card spreadsheet. Maintenance of stock cards will be done by admin office aide.
v.	At the present, CMI has no accountant of its own and relies solely to the Municipal Accountant. Therefore, CMI will be responsible in reporting stocks that were issued and received through the use of stock cards, RIS and SSMI. The recording function for the preparation of Journals and Costing will be endorsed to Accountant.
vi.	A weekly consolidation of the Reporting on Issuance of Supplies and Materials (RIS) and Summary of Supplies and Materials issued (SSMI) will be done to ensure that everything forwarded to admin aide was accounted for.
vii.	The consolidation will be submitted on a monthly or quarterly basis to Chief Accountant of the Municipality who shall counter check and compute cost of supplies issued and ending inventory using the moving average method.
viii.	Journal Entry will be prepared by Accountant and a supplies ledger card will be maintained by him. The Account can use the documents provided for basis in making the Journal Entries and Ledger items. 

d)The Candaba Municipal Infirmary will continue with the present practices of preparation and dissemination of demand letter to clients with outstanding balances and to copy furnish the accounting department as recommended by the Commission of Audit. 

e)An ordinance granting discount to municipal employees has already been enacted and approved.

f)An ordinance authorizing the mark-up on medicines is now under consideration.

However, for the Municipality to be considered fully disaster-prepared, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the (a) Acting-DRRMO to include in its plan, the gradual compliance with the minimum standards on disaster preparedness based on available resources; and (b) the Municipal Treasurer to transfer the unexpended/unobligated balance of the fund to the Special Trust Fund with actual cash transfer pursuant to COA Circular No. No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
The Municipal Planning and Development Officer is required to complete the update on the Comprehensive Land Use Plan (CLUP). To date, the CLUP is finished and undergoing public hearing and was submitted to Provincial Land Use Committee (PLUC) for review as of February 20, 2018.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive thru the MENRO (a) continue efforts in the scouting of qualified members to complete the required line up of the Solid Waste Management Board (SWMB); (b) continue with the linkage already started with the Liga ng mga Barangay in gaining support for the implementation of the ordinance on the proper segregation of reusable or recyclable materials from compostable wastes; (c) continue to accelerate efforts in the enforcement of “no segregation, no collection” policy; (d) pursue the establishment of the permanent MRF of the Municipality; and (e) continue to intensify and sustain the current information drive and awareness campaign on the regulations on solid waste management in coordination with the DepEd officials in the Municipality and barangay officials.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive continue with its efforts in implementing projects, programs and activities that are in line with the objectives of the Fund.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Budget Officer to adhere closely to the required allocation of 1% of IRA for the LCPC for the ensuing year or if not feasible, identify or attribute other programs of the Municipality where the ultimate beneficiaries are the same under the LCPC.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive (a) request from GSIS an assessment of premiums of the inventoried properties; (b) based on the assessment determine how much budget is needed to cover the insurance; (c) work out within the allotted budget the coverage of insurable properties giving priority to those exposed to highest risk of loss/damage thru fortuitous events; and (d) henceforth, continue to find ways to allocate budget, even if limited, for the Municipality’s insurable properties.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive request the (a) Sangguniang Bayan Office to (i) submit additional evidence/proof to substantiate the regularity of the said disbursements to prevent them from being suspended or disallowed in audit; (ii) limit or control their expenses for meals and observe prudence in utilizing government funds which are intended for public purpose and transact as far as practicable with registered businesses on transactions involving the office as a contribution to the tax generation of the Government (b) Budget Office to (i) ensure that expenses are charged to the appropriate account title; (ii) provide for an appropriation for Representation Expenses for meals and snacks served during meetings and conference instead of using Other Supplies Expense; and (c) Accounting Office to record the expenses to the appropriate account title such that meals and snacks served for purposes other than meetings and conferences, or other than those under the category of representation expenses shall be recorded as Miscellaneous Expenses or Other Maintenance and Operating Expenses, if warranted.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive  (a)  along with the Municipal Accountant and HRM Officers continue to abide with the documentary requirements on the payments of salaries under job order; (b) direct the Municipal Budget Officer to account in the Annual Budget the required amount of appropriation classified under Maintenance and Other Operating Expense (MOOE) for the salaries of job orders in the context of no “Lump sum appropriation” basis; and (b) instruct the HRMO to look for possible candidates from the array of job order employees for permanency based on available vacant positions for recommendation to the Personnel Selection Board (PSB) of the Municipality to reduce the number of job orders in the Municipality taking into consideration the mandated PS limitation under the Local Government Code.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) require the Municipal Planning and Development Officer (MPDO) in coordination with the GAD Focal Point person to continue working for the building up and completion of GAD database and the maintenance thereof in accordance with the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2013-01 dated July 18, 2013 for a more gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation.
We recommended that Management continue its efforts to collect receivables originating from the said disallowance.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive continue its relentless efforts in implementing the program through continuous feeding and monitoring of operations in aid of the sustained nutritional development of the target beneficiaries.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: (a) Communicate to the appropriate agencies involved in the Bottom-Up Budgeting and other future agencies the details on the correct trust fund account number of the municipality to lessen the burden of transferring from one account to another;

(b) Make an assessment of the volume of workload and the manpower needed in the Accounting Department and if necessary, cause the hiring of a qualified personnel (Certified Public Accountant) to handle the accounting functions of the Municipality or hire an additional staff who is a civil service eligible and a graduate of any accounting-related course preferable with experience in government accounting, who will assist the Acting Municipal Accountant in the processing of documents in the Municipality to achieve effective financial reporting; and regularly performs detailed analysis and review of cash accounts;

(c) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to (i) transfer the remaining balance of the trust fund to the trust fund account for easy monitoring (ii) consider the enrollment of the Municipality's bank accounts with the DBP EC Banking facility of the DBP, (iii) submit all paid disbursement vouchers summarized in the report of checks issued on a daily basis to ensure timely recording of disbursements in the books (iv) refrain from recording unreleased checks or checks still in custody at the end of every accounting period as credit to the cashbook balance so that only the amount of checks actually issued are recording in the cash book; and (v) properly monitor checks becoming stale to be able to recommend the proper recording of appropriate payable account; and

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive: a) Designate a separate Services Officer, in concurrent capacity, in the Municipal Infirmary to process all purchasing concerns and the keeping of records to track of the movement of stocks on hand from receipt until issuance for accounting, control and accountability purposes;
observations.managementAction: The Local Chief Executive gave instruction to the GSO to establish an effective procurement system compliant with the government regulations to ensure that all purchasing concerns of the Infirmary and other departments are properly recorded and monitored for a more efficient manner of accounting and control.

observations.recommendation: b. Consider the provision of storage so that the procurement of supplies in significant quantity can be stored to lessen the administrative cost in frequent procurements and disbursements; c. require the GSO to: (i) henceforth, prepare and submit documents on purchases of the Municipality; (ii) prepare and maintain stock cards for the purchased supplies (iiI) conduct periodic inventory of supplies as prescribed by the above regulation; and (iv) procure as far as practicable and feasible, directly from the DBM-PS all anticipated common-use supplies, and resort to local suppliers only upon unavailability of supplies needed;
observations.managementAction: The GSO’s attention was called upon to look and address the COA observation and he made note of the observations for future compliance particularly procurement of common use supplies with the DBP-PS.

observations.recommendation: c) require the Acting Municipal Accountant to: (i) ensure that the required documentary requirements supporting the purchase transactions are attached; (ii). Process only claims that are properly signed and approve/certified by the authorized officials; (iii). Henceforth recognize all purchase of supplies and materials under the proper inventory account (iv) prepare supplies ledger cards for delivered items as provided by the above-quotation.
observations.managementAction: The GSO and other concerned departments are required to strictly scrutinize the documentations, supporting documents, proper approval/certifications of authorized signatories before proceeding with any procurement. The Municipal Accountant is required to create proper accounts/books to properly record purchases of supplies/ inventories to avoid recording of said purchases as outright expense instead of inventories. Likewise, management is taking the necessary measure to assure that present procurements are properly recorded and inventoried for efficient control and proper accountabilities.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Executive: (a) instruct the Municipal Engineer to conduct an initial inventory of local road networks in the Municipality in reference to the procedure set by COA Circular 2015-008.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer was instructed the inventory of local road works.
observations.reason: Unavailable budget, limited personnel and vast land area of the Municipality hindered the start of the inventory.

observations.recommendation: (b) require the GSO and the Acting Municipal Accountant to: (i) prepare and maintain Property Ledger Cards and Property Card, starting with the current year’s acquisition and recent years’ acquisitions and aim to increase the coverage until all PPE items are covered; (ii) reconcile inventory results with property records and those maintained at the Accounting Office and isolate items not reconciled for easy reference in succeeding inventory taking (iii) aim to reduce the number and amount of unreconciled PPE account balances; c. mobilize additional personnel to facilitate the completion of lacking data count of PPE items and in the event that documents needed to obtain costing of PPE is no longer feasible, consider the fair value thereof as point of reference pursuant to PPSAS 17; d. Strengthen the controls on the handling and recording procedures for the PPE by performing a periodic reconciliation of the records of the accounting, the property and actual inventory results;
observations.managementAction: The preparation of property card and ledger cards were not facilitated prior to the present administration because of the lack of proper turnover on the inventory of property when GSO assumed office. The GSO was given clear instruction to comply with the recommendation of the COA on proper recording of PPE inventory. All acquisitions henceforth should be properly recorded and reflected in the PPE account.
observations.reason: The preparation of property card and ledger cards were not facilitated prior to the present administration because of the lack of proper turnover on the inventory of property when GSO assumed office.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the: (a) Local Chief Executive and Local Finance Committee (1) allot adequate budget for the payment of premiums on the insurable properties of the Municipality;

(b) GSO to (i) conduct an inventory and assessment of all insurable properties taking note of those that are exposed to high risk to disasters from casualty, thus giving priority to these assets in the availment of insurance; and (ii) ensure the properties identified are duly covered with insurance from the GSIS to pave the way for indemnification in case of disasters and the like
(b) from the submitted PPMP’s require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Secretariat to consolidate the latter as basis in the preparation of the Annual Procurement Plan (APP).

© cause the GSO to: (i) post procurement activities in the PhilGEPS except those that are specifically exempted for posting as mentioned above; (ii) furnish COA copies of all purchase order/contract and all papers relative thereto within five (5) days after the perfection of the contract and sending notification of the time and date of the scheduled deliveries to the Office of the Auditor pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001 and 95-006; (iii) strictly monitor the completion of data to be supplied to the PO more particularly the date of delivery and the date of receipt of PO by the supplier in order to determine any delay in delivery and impose the corresponding penalty.
observations.reason: Posting with PhilGEPS was strictly adhered to by the Municipality. However, the furnishing of contract on time was not facilitated during the year.

observations.recommendation: (d) require the Municipal Engineer or the Project Engineer, if any, to strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular 2013-004 specifically in posting the required information on tarpaulins/billboard to promote good governance through transparency and accountability to the people.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the officials concerned, such as the members of the BAC, Municipal Accountant and General Services to: (a) submit the required documents as discussed above in accordance with RA No. 9184 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 for further evaluation as basis for the Audit Team’s subsequent audit action;
(b) require the Municipal Accountant to check the completeness of the required supporting documents for every transactions before any payment is made;
(c) henceforth, adopt competitive bidding on transactions above the threshold of P200,000.00 in accordance with the requirements set by 2016 RIRR of RA No. 9184.
(d) submit written explanation on the noted deficiencies in the documentation of the payment of claims.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive (a) provide for the budget and cause acquisition of the lacking facilities and other improvements to address the requirements of DOH so as not to disrupt the delivery of health services to the needy constituents;
observations.managementAction: The management is desirous in its vision to provide the basic health services to the needy constituents of Candaba, hence, the LGU has taken steps to ensure that the facilities and other improvements needed in the infirmary are addressed. Instructions were given to the GSO to ensure that the facilities that required repair and/or maintenance be given upmost priority.
observations.reason: The lacking facilities are to be incorporated on the proposed extension building of the Infirmary

observations.recommendation: (b) require the Municipal Treasurer to transfer to a trust fund account the remaining PhilHealth benefits received from prior years and henceforth deposit all upcoming receipts to the trust fund account for easy monitoring and evaluation of disbursements made with the fund

(c) Municipal Accountant and Budget officer: i. follow strictly the accounting and budgetary treatment on trust receipts; ii. strictly monitor disbursements from the PCF taking note of the allowed charges to the fund as prescribed in the Philhealth Circular.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive and the General Services Officer instruct all drivers concerned to prepare and submit Driver’s Trip Ticket, Monthly Report of Official Travel and Report of Fuel Consumption and other required procedures in reference to the Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption. Accordingly, these reports must also be submitted to the Office of the Auditor on a monthly basis.
observations.reason: All drivers did not comply with the directives on preparation of trip tickets.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) instruct the BAC members, Municipal Engineer and MPDC to properly plan and schedule all procurement activities pertaining to development projects to pave the way for their timely implementation and completion; (b) ensure that the Municipal Planning and Development Officer and Municipal Engineer regularly monitor the status of project implementation so that necessary interventions may be introduced at the earliest time; and (c) strictly utilize the 20% Development Fund to only eligible capital expenditures to ensure that the Fund is utilized only for its intended purpose.
observations.managementAction:
The present administration is not privy to the preparation of the CY 2016 Budget as well as the utilization of the 20% Development Fund prior to assumption of office.
However, as pointed out by the MPDO in the identified programs and projects are to be implemented for the succeeding years being part of the 20% Development Fund as a continuing appropriation.
observations.reason: Implementation of the prior year’s continuing appropriations were prioritized in the implementation after which the current year. However, compliance with the required timelines in the bidding was the identified reason of the deviation owing to the number of projects to be implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive: (a) Fill in the required member of the MDRRMC specially one from the private sector and one from the Philippine Red Cross, as members of the council.

(b) encourage personnel of the Municipality to be concurrent members of the emergency response team to provide adequate assistance to the constituents during times of calamities and to provide necessary technical trainings to the members by attending drills and trainings organized by different government agencies.

(d) require the MDRRMO to prepare the LDRRMFIP and Monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of the LDRMMF within the prescribed period; and facilitate monitoring of the accomplishment vis-à-vis target projects for effective and efficient utilization of the Fund.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) institutionalize a working and functional GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) or a similar GAD mechanism to catalyze and accelerate gender mainstreaming within the Agency; (b) encourage and direct the GFPS Committee and Technical Working Group to perform their mandated tasks; and (c) require the Municipal Planning and Development Officer (MPDO) to conduct studies relative to the establishment of the GAD database and initiate its organization to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation.

(b) through the Liga ng mga Barangay, encourage active participation of barangay officials to support the solid waste management program of the government by educating their respective constituents on the proper segregation of reusable or recyclable materials from compostable waste.
(c) ensure that the existing MRF of the 16 component barangays are fully functional and provide necessary support in enjoining the remaining 17 barangays to establish their own MRF;
observations.reason: As of this writing, reports showed an increase in the percentage of compliant Barangays.

observations.recommendation: (d) strictly implement applicable ordinances relative to solid waste management program;
(e) Initiate parallel efforts with the barangay officials in the implementation of the basic environmental laws no preserving and protecting the environmental required under RA No. 9003

(f) intensify and sustain the current information drive and awareness campaign on the regulations on solid waste management in coordination with the DepEd officials in the Municipality and barangays officials.
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the concerned officials to submit the required documents as discussed above, for further evaluation.

We recommended that  the Local  Chief  Executive: 

(a) make representation with the depository bank for the  prompt release of bank statements  to the Acting Municipal Accountant so that Monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements are prepared for the early detection of errors and the correction thereof  both in the books of the Municipality and the bank; and 




(b)  instruct the Municipal Accountant  to enroll the current accounts of  the Municipality with the “we access” facility of the depository bank or its equivalent allowing the online generation of statement of accounts at any date.
observations.reason: The preparation of Bank Reconciliation Statement could not be made by the Acting Municipal Accountant due to the volume of work that he handles and lack of manpower to assist in him in his duties as Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our last year’s recommendation that the Local Chief  Executive instruct the: Municipal  Accountant to strictly follow the perpetual inventory method  in accounting for supplies and maintain supplies ledger cards for each item which should be reconciled with the stock cards maintained by the designated Supply/Property Officer; General Services Officer  to prepare and maintain stock cards for each items and support issuance of supplies with requisition and issuance slip (RIS).

We reiterated our prior years’ recommendation that the members of the BAC recommend the alternative mode of procurement of  common-use supplies directly from the Procurement Service in compliance with Section 1 of A.O. No. 17, s. 2011 and Section 21 of the General Provisions of the General Appropriation Act FY 2015.

We reiterated our prior years’ recommendation that the Local  Chief  Executive  instruct the concerned officials to submit/furnish the Audit Team copies of contracts, purchase orders and all supporting documents forming part thereof within the prescribed  period pursuant  to COA Circular Nos. 96-010 and 2009-001.  

We also recommended that  notices of deliveries be submitted  to the Auditor  within the prescribed period  to conduct timely inspection of  deliveries on  a sampling  basis  pursuant to COA Circular No. 95-006 dated May 18, 1995.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer and the General Services Officer strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 on the information and publicity on the PPAs of the Government Agencies to promote good governance through transparency and accountability to the people.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2017, all infrastructure projects were required to be posted with tarpaulin/bill-boards.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior years’ recommendation that Management strictly adhere to the provisions of RA 9184 in all its procurement activities to attain the government’s effort to promote the principle of transparency, efficiency and economy on the procurement system.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2017, all purchases were posted in the PhilGEPS, except those specifically exempted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended  that management require the LDRRMO to prepare a comprehensive and integrated LDRRMIP for a more effective way of  addressing disaster  risk reduction and  management system which  should  be (i) patterned  in accordance with the NDRRMP and/or PDRRM Framework as required by RA 10121 and to be validated and accustomed  to the local needs for  prevention and  mitigation, preparedness, response, rehabilitation and recovery; and (ii) in accordance with the prescribed format in COA Circular No. 2012-002; and
instruct the Acting Accountant to submit the required LDRRMF utilization report within the prescribed  period.
observations.reason: Familiarization of the Acting MDRRMO on the requirements of various laws and regulations relative to disaster risk reduction.

observations.recommendation:
1.4. We recommended that Local Chief Executive
a. Communicate to the appropriate agencies concerned involved in
Bottom-Up Budgeting and other future partner agencies the details on the
correct trust fund account number of the municipality to lessen the burden of
transferring funds from one account to another;
b. Make an assessment of the volume of workload and the manpower
needed in the Accounting Department and if necessary, cause the hiring of a
qualified personnel (Certified Public Accountant) to handle the accounting
functions of the Municipality or hire an additional staff who is a civil service
eligible and a graduate of any accounting-related course preferably with
experience in government accounting, who will assist the Acting Municipal
Accountant in the processing of documents in the Municipality to achieve
effective financial reporting; and regularly perform detailed analysis and
review of cash accounts;
c. instruct the Municipal Treasurer to (i) transfer the remaining balance
of trust fund to the trust fund account for easy monitoring; (ii) consider the
enrollment of the Municipalitys bank accounts with the DBP EC Banking
facility of the Development Bank of the Philippines (DBP), an online based
banking facility which allows online generation of statement of accounts at
any date and may also provide processing of transactions via the internet;
(iii) submit all paid disbursement vouchers summarized in the report of
checks issued on a daily basis to ensure timely recording of disbursements in
the books; (iv) refrain from recording unreleased checks or checks still in
custody at the end of every accounting period as credit to the cashbook
balance so that only the amount of checks actually issued are recorded in the
cashbook; and (v) properly monitor checks becoming stale to be able to
recommend the proper recording of appropriate payable account; and
d. instruct the Acting Municipal Accountant to (i) initiate the conduct of
reconciliation of the Cash- in- Bank records with the depository bank within
ten (10) days from receipt of the Bank Statements and with the Treasurers
cash records and (ii) submit the same to the auditor within the prescribed
period in consonance to Item 3.2. of COA Circular 96-001.

2.4. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a) designate a separate General Services Officer, in concurrent capacity,
in the Candaba Municipal Infirmary to process all purchasing concerns and
the keeping of records to keep track of the movement of stocks on hand from
receipt until issuance for accounting, control and accountability purposes.
b) consider the provision of storage so that the procurement of supplies
in significant quantity can be stored to lessen the administrative cost in
frequent procurements and reimbursements.
c) require the General Services Officer to (i) henceforth, prepare and
submit documents on purchases of the Municipality ; (ii) prepare and
maintain stock cards for the purchased supplies; (iii) conduct periodic
inventory of supplies as prescribed by the above regulation; and (iv) procure,
as far as practicable and feasible, directly from the DBM-PS all anticipated
common-use supplies, and resort to local suppliers only upon unavailability
of supplies needed; and
d) require the Acting Municipal Accountant to (i) ensure that the
required documentary requirements supporting the purchase transactions
are attached; (ii) process only claims that are properly signed and
approved/certified by the authorized officials; (iii) henceforth recognize all
purchase of supplies and materials under the proper inventory account; (ii)
prepare supplies ledger cards for delivered items as provided by the above
quoted regulation.

3.4. We recommended that the Local Executive
(a) instruct the Municipal Engineer to conduct an initial inventory of
local road networks in the Municipality in reference to the procedure set by
COA Circular 2015-008;
(b) require the General Services Officer and the Acting Municipal
Accountant to (i) prepare and maintain Property Ledger Cards and Property
Cards, starting with the current years acquisition and recent years
acquisitions and aim to increase the coverage until all PPE items are covered;
(ii) reconcile inventory results with property records and those maintained at
the Accounting Office and isolate items not reconciled for easy reference in
succeeding inventory taking; (iii) aim to reduce the number and amount of
unreconciled PPE account balances; and
(c) mobilize additional personnel to facilitate the completion of lacking
data on physical count of PPE items and in the event that documents needed
to obtain costing of PPE is no longer feasible, consider the fair value thereof
as point of reference pursuant to PPSAS 17; (d) strengthen the controls on
the handling and recording procedures for the PPE by performing a periodic
reconciliation of the records of the accounting, the property and actual
inventory results.

4.11. We recommended that the (a) Local Chief Executive and Local Finance
Committee (a) allot adequate budget for the payment of premiums on the insurable
properties of the Municipality;( b) General Services Officer (i) conduct an inventory
and assessment of all insurable properties taking note of those that are exposed to
high risk to disasters; from casualty, thus giving priority on these assets in the
availment of insurance; and (ii ) ensure that properties identified are duly covered
with insurance from the GSIS to pave the way for indemnification in case of
disasters and the like.

5.3. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a) instruct end users or implementing units in the next budgeting period
to prepare Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) as basis of the
budget for the ensuing year;
b) from the submitted PPMPs require the Bids and Awards Committee
(BAC) Secretariat to consolidate the latter as basis in the preparation of the
Annual Procurement Plan (APP);
c) cause the General Services Officer (GSO) to (i) post procurement
activities in the PhilGEPS except those that are specifically exempted for
posting as mentioned above; and (ii) furnish COA copies of all purchase
orders/contracts and all papers relative thereto within five (5) days after
the perfection of the contract and sending of notification of the time and
date of the scheduled deliveries to the Office of the Auditor pursuant to
COA Circular No. 2009-001 and 95-006; (iii) strictly monitor the completion
of data to be supplied in the PO more importantly the date of delivery and
the date of receipt of PO by the supplier in order to determine any delay in
delivery; and
d) require the Municipal Engineer or the Project Engineer, if any, to
strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular 2013-004 specifically in
posting the required information on tarpaulins/billboards to promote good
governance through transparency and accountability to the people.

6.16. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the officials
concerned, such as the members of the BAC, Municipal Accountant, and General
Services Officer to:
a) submit the required documents as discussed above in accordance with
RA No. 9184 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 for further evaluation as
basis for the Audit Teams subsequent audit action;
b) require the Municipal Accountant to check the completeness of the
required supporting documents for every transaction before any payment
is made;
c) henceforth, adopt competitive bidding on transactions above the
threshold of 00,000.00 in accordance with the requirements set by the
2016 RIRR of RA No. 9184; and
d) submit written explanation on the noted deficiencies in the
documentation of the payment of claims.

7.7. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive consider hiring additional
accounting staff to (a) maintain the Special Accounts in the General Fund; and (b)
prepare the corresponding schedules to support the financial statements which will
serve as tools in informing Management in evaluating the viability of the economic
enterprise operated by the Municipality.

8.8. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) provide for the budget
and cause the acquisition of the lacking facilities and other improvements to address
the requirements of DOH AO NO. 2012-0012 so as not to disrupt the delivery of
health services to the needy constituents; (b) require the Municipal Treasurer to
transfer to a trust fund account the remaining PhilHealth benefits received from
prior years and henceforth deposit all forthcoming receipts to the trust fund account
for easy monitoring and evaluation of disbursements made with the fund; (c)
Municipal Accountant and Budget Officer (i) follow strictly the accounting and
budgetary treatment on trust receipts; and (iii) strictly monitor disbursements from
the PCF taking note of the allowed charges to the fund as prescribed in PhilHealth
Circular No. 16, series 2004 dated July 12, 2004.

9.5. We recommended that Local Chief Executive (LCE) and the General
Services Officer instruct all drivers concerned to prepare and submit Drivers Trip
Ticket, Monthly Report of Official Travel and Report of Fuel Consumption and
other required procedures in reference to the Manual on Audit for Fuel
Consumption. Accordingly, these reports must also be submitted to the office of the
Auditor on a monthly basis.

10.10. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) instruct the BAC
members, Municipal Engineer and MPDC to properly plan and schedule all
procurement activities pertaining to development projects to pave the way for their
timely implementation and completion; (b) ensure that the Municipal Planning and
Development Officer and Municipal Engineer regularly monitor the status of
project implementation so that necessary interventions may be introduced at the
earliest time; and (c) strictly utilize the 20% Development Fund to only eligible
capital expenditures to ensure that the Fund is utilized only for its intended
purpose.

11.15. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive
a. fill in the required member of the MDRRMC specifically one from the
private sector and one from the Philippine Red Cross, as members of the
Council;
b. encourage personnel of the Municipality to be concurrent members of the
emergency response team to provide adequate assistance to the
constituents during times of calamities and to provide necessary technical
trainings to the members by attending drills and trainings organized by
different government agencies;
c. require the Municipal Treasurer to transfer the unexpended/unobligated
balance of the fund to the Special Trust Fund with actual cash transfer
pursuant to COA Circular No. No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012;
and
d. require the MDRRMO to prepare the LDRRMFIP and Monthly Report
on the Sources and Utilization of the LDRMMF within the prescribed
period; and facilitate monitoring of the accomplishment vis-vis targeted
projects for effective and efficient utilization of the Fund.

12.12. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) institutionalize a
working and functional GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) or a similar GAD
mechanism to catalyze and accelerate gender mainstreaming within the Agency; (b)
encourage and direct the GFPS Committee and Technical Working Group to
perform their mandated tasks; and (c) require the Municipal Planning and
Development Officer (MPDO) to conduct studies relative to the establishment of the
GAD database and initiate its organization to serve as basis for gender-responsive
planning, programming and policy formulation.

13.9. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a) evaluate the current composition of the MSWMB and look for possible
members that could qualify to complete the line up;
b) through the Liga ng mga Barangay, encourage active participation of
barangay officials to support the solid waste management program of the
government by educating their respective constituents on the proper
segregation of reusable or recyclable materials from compostable wastes;
c) ensure that the existing MRF of the 16 component barangays are fully
functional and provide necessary support in enjoining the remaining 17
barangays to establish their own MRF;
d) strictly implement applicable ordinances relative to solid waste
management programs;
e) initiate parallel efforts with the barangay officials in the implementation
of the five basic environmental laws on preserving and protecting the
environment required under R.A. No. 9003; and
f) intensify and sustain the current information drive and awareness
campaign on the regulations on solid waste management in coordination
with the DepEd officials in the Municipality and barangay officials.

14.10. We recommended that the Members of the Local School Board:
a) prepare a balanced budget with conscious and concerted efforts of
observing compliance with the legal and regulatory requirements on
SEF budgeting, allocation and utilization in reference to the
abovementioned rules and regulations and the latest 2017 guidelines;
b) maximize the utilization of SEF to attain its objectives, monitor the
implementation of the PPAs, and take appropriate actions such as
realignment or reprograming, if necessary; and
c) submit to the Audit Team the required documentations as enumerated
above and henceforth, comply with the required documentation for
similar transactions in the future.

15.15. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the concerned
officials to submit the required documents as discussed above, for further evaluation
and subsequent audit action.

16.5. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive continue to monitor
payments of membership fee/dues to leagues duly authorized by the Local
Government Code such as the leagues of local government units and leagues of
elective officials.

19.6. We recommended that Management continue its efforts to collect receivables
originating from the said disallowance.

We recommended that the Local Executive instruct the inventory committee to conduct inventory  by Office to facilitate the completion of  physical inventory  of all  property, plant and equipment of the Municipality; require the General Services Officer and the Acting Municipal  Accountant to (i) update and complete Property Records and Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger cards, respectively, starting with the current year’s acquisition and recent years’ acquisitions and aim to increase the coverage until all PPE items are covered; (ii) reconcile inventory results with property records and  those maintained  at the Accounting Office and isolate items  not reconciled for easy reference in succeeding  inventory taking; (iii) aim to reduce the number and amount of unreconciled  PPE account balances; and direct the General Services Officer to update  Acknowledgement Receipts for  Equipment (ARE) for  all PPE items, starting  with the current and recent years’ acquisition  until all items are covered and  there after , renew the AREs at least every  three years or  whenever  there is transfer of accountability.
observations.reason: An actual inventory was conducted by the General Services Officer (GSO) as at year end. However, it did not include cost of each PPE owing to the lack of the required information to complete the report and reconcile the same with the accounting records.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended  that the Local  Chief  Executive

(a) coordinate  efforts with the barangay  officials  on the implementation of  the five basic environmental  laws on preserving  and  protecting  the environment  required  under R.A. No. 9003;  

(b) initiate intensive information  drive and awareness campaign on the regulations on solid waste management in coordination  with the DepEd  officials in the Municipality and  barangay officials; and 

(c) strictly enforce the ordinances on “no segregation, no collection” and “No Littering” for the total.
observations.reason: Constituents’ lack/limited drive to follow the provisions of the Law.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management continue to implement more programs and projects which partake the nature of investments and capital expenditures for the benefit and well-being of  the communities and  its constituents.

We recommended  that management:

(a) set aside at least 5% of the appropriated  budget as required in Section 36 of RA 9710 for  integration on  GAD-related projects, programs and activities; and

(b) require the focal  person on GAD to prepare and submit to the Audit  Team the GAD Plan and Accomplishment Report.

We recommended that management set aside at least 1% of its budget for the plans, programs, project and activities for senior citizens and differently-abled persons pursuant to Section 2 of DBM-DSWD Joint Circular No. 2003-1 dated  April 28, 2003.
